summary: This is a very brief teaser post promoting Joel's longer article 'Hitting the High Notes.' Joel poses rhetorical questions about whether the variation in programmer talent is significant enough to matter. He suggests that while the answer may seem obvious to some, many people still need convincing that the best programmers are dramatically better than average ones.
key_points: There is enormous variation in programmer ability, and it matters significantly
The importance of hiring the best programmers is not universally accepted and needs to be argued
This post serves as a teaser for the full 'Hitting the High Notes' essay
money_quotes: Does it even make sense to talk about having the "best programmers?" Is there so much variation between programmers that this even matters?
Maybe it's obvious to us, but to many, the assertion still needs to be proven.
key_insight: The vast difference in capability between the best and average programmers is a reality that still needs to be demonstrated to many people.
